name: AXS Delayed Retest Continuation v1
thesis: AXS 1h is the structurally different lane for testing breakout continuation with delayed entry after retest rather than immediate trigger. The edge should come from pullback acceptance after the break, not from chasing the first impulse.
invalidation_condition: Abort if retests are too rare, if retest and continuation filters conflict, or if the second leg fails to survive costs.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: A breakout occurs, price retests rather than extending immediately, and continuation resumes only if the retest holds.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Many failed breakout ideas enter too early; delaying until retest acceptance can improve participation quality and reduce whipsaw risk.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: AXS 1h is a useful structurally different lane because it should show obvious pullback-and-resume behavior when the second leg is real, while still stress-testing the thesis on a more reactive asset.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If AXS behaves as pure noise rather than structured retests, the continuation leg may never materialize after entry.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries occur but continuation fades too quickly, tighten the time-based de-risk before making retest depth stricter.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; retest hold and DI confirmation are aligned
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: kept reasonable by broad retest-distance requirements

long: Time-based de-risk after 3 bars if unrealized gain < 0.2 * ATR_14
short: Time-based de-risk after 3 bars if unrealized gain < 0.2 * ATR_14
